response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong software-craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems), but their stack is overwhelmingly DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript. C++ exposure is minimal and dated (LabWindows/CVI components and a bachelor-thesis project), and the skills list omits C++ entirely. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT/REST IoT work, secure development per IEC 62443, or industrial-automation protocols, which are central to this role. Despite ample overall experience, the mismatch on the core C++ and industrial-cybersecurity competencies makes this a weak fit.
strengths: Broad senior experience with strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency, multi-threading via DLang fibers)
Some C/C++ background (NI LabWindows/CVI components, C++ wave-field-synthesis thesis)
Proven ownership and communication via talks, workshops, and open-source platform leadership
concerns: C++/STL is not a core or recent skill; primary stack is DLang, Node.js, TypeScript
No demonstrated experience with MQTT, message brokers, IoT, or industrial automation protocols
No secure-development-lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity exposure
